channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Haitians face record hunger as gang violence grips country in throes of economic crisis
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — More than half of Haiti’s population is expected to experience severe hunger through June, and another 8,400 people living in makeshift shelters are projected to starve, according to a new report released this week.
item.dc:creator: EVENS SANON and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN nuclear watchdog says US-Iran talks at a 'very crucial' stage
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Talks between Iran and the United States over Tehran's rapidly advancing nuclear program are “in a very crucial” stage, the head of the United Nations' nuclear watchdog said Thursday while on a visit to the Islamic Republic.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and AMIR VAHDAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis visits Rome prison during Holy Week
item.description: Pope Francis kept annual Holy Week appointment visiting dozens of inmates in a Rome prison on Thursday. The 88-year-old is still recovering from pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia strikes Ukrainian city of Dnipro as Kyiv seeks security pledges in Paris
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian forces launched a massive drone attack on the southeastern Ukrainian city of Dnipro, killing three people, including a child, and wounding dozens, officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Cable car accident in southern Italy kills at least 4 people
item.description: Officials in southern Italy say that a cable car carrying tourists south of Naples has crashed after the cable snapped, killing at least four people and critically injuring one.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: ICC opens inquiry into Hungary for failing to arrest Netanyahu
item.description: Judges at the International Criminal Court have asked Hungary to explain why it failed to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu when he visited Budapest earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Molly Quell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump confident he'll make tariff deal with EU, China amid Meloni visit
item.description: President Trump on Thursday predicted his administration would make a deal on trade with China and with the European Union amid tariff disputes.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Former FAA contractor pleads guilty to sharing US airport files with Iran
item.description: A former Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) contractor pleaded guilty Wednesday to working as an agent of Iran and sharing sensitive U.S. airport data with Iranian intelligence officers. Abouzar Rahmati, 42, admitted to conspiring to act and acting as an agent of Iran on U.S.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Noem threatens Harvard with ban on foreign student visas
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem threatened Wednesday to ban Harvard University from taking in foreign students amid a growing battle over the Ivy League school not accepting the demands of the Trump administration to change its policies.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: Former MLS player Aaron Boupendza dies after falling from building, soccer officials say
item.description: The Gabonese soccer federation announced Wednesday that Aaron Boupendza, a striker for its men's national team, has died after falling from the 11th floor of a building in China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A Russian bucket brigade helps toads and frogs cross the road to get to a spawning site
item.description: A NATURE RESERVE NEAR SESTRORETSK, Russia (AP) — It happens every spring along a section of road north of Russia's second-largest city of St. Petersburg: Volunteers, many in yellow vests, patrol near the Sestroretsk Bog nature reserve.
item.dc:creator: KIRILL ZARUBIN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Artisans in Guatemala hand-stitch velvet cloaks for Holy Week processions
item.description: SAN MIGUEL ESCOBAR, Guatemala (AP) — Stitch by stitch, artisans have worked for months on the elaborate garments that will debut this week in Holy Week processions across Guatemala and beyond.
item.dc:creator: SONIA PÉREZ D., Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Power blackout hits all of Puerto Rico ahead of Easter weekend
item.description: All 1.4 million customers were affected. By late Wednesday night, crews had restored power to 175,000 customers, or 12%.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he's in 'no rush' to end tariffs as he meets with Italy's Meloni
item.description: President Donald Trump says he's in “no rush” to reach any trade deals because he views tariffs as making the United States wealthy.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Barry, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Carney says Trump key issue in Canada's election, while Conservative rival says country needs change
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Prime Minister Mark Carney said Wednesday the key question in Canada's election is who is best to deal with U.S. President Donald Trump while his Conservative rival argued that Carney doesn't represent change after a decade of Liberal Party rule.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian strikes on Ukraine's Kherson kill 1 person and wound 9
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian glide bombs and artillery struck a city in southern Ukraine on Wednesday, killing one person and wounding nine others as Moscow forces continued daily attacks across the country.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Dog reunites with Israeli family after disappearing for 18 months in Gaza
item.description: BINYAMINA, Israel (AP) — Rachel Dancyg never thought she would see her dog again after it disappeared in the Hamas attack that sparked the ongoing war with Israel. Her ex-husband and brother were abducted from Kibbutz Nir Oz in southern Israel and killed.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Puerto Rico goes dark after massive power outage
item.description: Puerto Rico went dark on Wednesday after experiencing a massive power outage on the island impacting families, businesses and homes alike.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: UN envoy urges Security Council to try to prevent renewed civil war in South Sudan
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The top United Nations official in South Sudan urged the U.N. Security Council on Wednesday to use its clout to prevent the world’s newest nation from again plunging into civil war.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Maryland Sen. Van Hollen says he was denied entry to the El Salvador prison holding Abrego Garcia
item.description: Maryland Sen. Chris Van Hollen says he was denied entry into the El Salvador prison where Kilmar Abrego Garcia is being held.
item.dc:creator: Mary Clare Jalonick And Matthew Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: UK's top court says definition of a woman is based on biological sex and excludes transgender people
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The U.K. Supreme Court ruled Wednesday that a woman is someone born biologically female, excluding transgender people from the legal definition in a long-running dispute between a feminist group and the Scottish government.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, JILL LAWLESS and SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: Grassley pressed on wrongly deported man in heated exchange at town hall
item.description: Sen. Chuck Grassley (R-Iowa) was pressed on the status of Kilmar Abrego Garcia, a Maryland man who was wrongly deported to El Salvador, during a heated exchange at a town hall he held Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, The Hill

item.title: Tens of thousands of Jews gather for traditional blessing in Jerusalem
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Tens of thousands of people gathered at the holiest Jewish prayer site in Jerusalem on Tuesday for the traditional priestly blessing. The “Birkat Cohanim,” or Cohen’s blessing, is a ritual dating back over 2,500 years to when King Solomon’s Temple stood on the same site.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top Trump officials will hold talks with Europeans on the Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Steve Witkoff, President Donald Trump’s special envoy, will travel to Paris this week for talks with European allies on U.S. efforts to negotiate a peace deal between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Iran confirms that the 2nd round of nuclear talks with the US will be in Rome
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Iran confirmed Wednesday that the next round of nuclear talks with the United States this weekend will be held in Rome after earlier confusion over where the negotiations would be held.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and NASSER KARIMI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Soros v Trump: Socialists targets conservatives in upcoming European nation's election
item.description: Ahead of elections in May, Albania’s PM Edi Rama has been accused of targeting opposition candidates. One opposition figures says the influence of President Trump, George and Alex Soros will be felt.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shootout rescues Tennessee pastor abducted in South Africa
item.description: Josh Sullivan, a self-described “church-planting missionary,” has been reunited with his wife and their two children.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Russia-linked hackers targeting European diplomats with invites to bogus wine tasting events
item.description: A Russia-linked hacking group is trying to get European diplomats to download malicious software through invitations to fake wine tasting events, a report says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In the Ukrainian city of Sumy, life goes on despite the constant threat of attack
item.description: SUMY, Ukraine (AP) — The humdrum of daily life in Sumy belies the constant threat of death its people have lived with since Russia invaded Ukraine three years ago.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and YEHOR KONOVALOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hong Kong post office will stop shipping parcels to US over tariffs
item.description: The U.S. earlier announced it would end a customs exception allowing small-value parcels from Hong Kong to enter the U.S. without tax.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: NATO chief reaffirms support for Ukraine during a visit to the port city of Odesa
item.description: ODESA, Ukraine (AP) — NATO’s support for Ukraine remains “unwavering,” the alliance’s secretary-general said Tuesday, emphasizing that more than 20 billion euros — over $22 billion — in security assistance has already been pledged by NATO allies in the first three months of the year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump floats deporting 'homegrown criminals' to El Salvador
item.description: Trump has suggested shipping off U.S. citizens who commit violent crimes to places like El Salvador's notorious mega-prison CECOT.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: More than 30 Ukrainians killed by Russian ballistics in Palm Sunday strike
item.description: Russia hit the Ukrainian city of Sumy with two missiles as civilian celebrated Palm Sunday, killing more than 30 people and injuring dozens of others, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
